content.title: Hobson
content.description:
Hobson is a live programming environment where everything is represented uniformly and is editable by the user. This includes the kernel the runs the system, and all of the userland developed features including the entire UI. The system runs entirely in your browser, persisted in IndexedDB, with no server required.

---

## Getting Started

1. Open [Bootloader](item://f761e023-da8d-492d-9933-a75adcc8d316) in a browser
2. On first run, you'll see an import prompt: *"Hobson needs a kernel to run"*
3. Use the [kernel-export](item://aaaa0001-1000-0000-0000-000000000000) library to create new import file from your running instance.
4. Select the JSON file generated in step 3.
5. The system reloads and you're live

After the initial import, Hobson persists everything in IndexedDB—edit items, create views, write code, all within Hobson itself.

**Key shortcuts:**
- `Esc` or `Ctrl+\` — Open REPL console
- `Ctrl+K` — Item search/navigate
- `Ctrl+Shift+S` — Safe mode (emergency recovery)

---

## Philosophy

Hobson draws from several traditions in interactive computing:

- **Conway's Humane Dozen** — Immediate, inspectable, modifiable interfaces
- **Smalltalk** — Image-based persistence where the environment is the application
- **Lisp** — REPL-first development, code as data
- **Obenauer's Itemized OS** — Universal containers and graph-based organization
- **Obsidian** — Bidirectional linking and transclusion

For the full exploration of these ideas, see [Philosophy & Inspirations](item://a0a0a0a0-d0c0-4000-8000-000000000001).

---

## Documentation

### Conceptual

- [Core Concepts](item://a0a0a0a0-d0c0-4000-8000-000000000002) — Items, types, code as data, views
- [Architecture Overview](item://a0a0a0a0-d0c0-4000-8000-000000000003) — Bootstrap, kernel modules, boot process

### Technical

- [Views & Rendering](item://a0a0a0a0-d0c0-4000-8000-000000000004) — View lookup, renderer API, partial re-rendering
- [Spatial Windowing](item://ef793c27-2d4b-4c99-b05a-2769db5bc5a9)  — 2D canvas, window management, sibling containers
- [Events System](item://a0a0a0a0-d0c0-4000-8000-000000000006) — Type-hierarchy dispatch, declarative watches

---

## Kernel Modules

The kernel is decomposed into focused subsystems:

```query
const modules = await api.query({ type: api.IDS.KERNEL_MODULE });
modules.sort((a, b) => (a.name || '').localeCompare(b.name || ''));
return transcludeEach(modules, m =>
  `- [${m.name}](item://${m.id}) — ${m.content?.description?.split('\n')[0] || 'No description'}`
);
```

---

## Views

Views render items. The system discovers views by type and preference:

```query
const views = await api.query({ type: api.IDS.VIEW });
const userViews = views.filter(v => v.content?.displayName);
userViews.sort((a, b) => (a.content?.displayName || '').localeCompare(b.content?.displayName || ''));
return transcludeEach(userViews, v =>
  `- **${v.content.displayName}** ([${v.name}](item://${v.id})) — ${v.content.description?.split('\n')[0] || 'No description'}`
);
```

---

## Libraries

Reusable code modules loaded via `api.require('name')`:

```query
const libs = await api.query({ type: api.IDS.LIBRARY });
libs.sort((a, b) => (a.name || '').localeCompare(b.name || ''));
return transcludeEach(libs, lib =>
  `- [${lib.name}](item://${lib.id}) — ${lib.content?.description?.split('\n')[0] || 'No description'}`
);
```

---

## Seed Items

Foundational items with well-known GUIDs:

![kernel:core SEED_IDS](item://33333333-1111-0000-0000-000000000000#code?region=SEED_IDS)

---

## Safe Mode

If userland code breaks the system, add `?safe=1` to the URL (or press `Ctrl+Shift+S`). Safe mode boots only the kernel and provides:

- Item browser with raw JSON editing
- Export/import for backup and recovery
- System reset option

See [kernel:safe-mode](item://33333333-7777-0000-0000-000000000000) for implementation.

---

## Quick Reference

| Task | How |
|------|-----|
| Search items | `Ctrl+P` or `api.showItemList()` |
| Edit JSON | Right-click → Edit JSON, or `api.editItemRaw('id')` |
| Export all | `await api.export()` |
| Import | `await api.import()` |
| Navigate | `await api.navigate('item-id')` |
| Query | `await api.query({ type: api.IDS.VIEW })` |
| Create | `await api.set({ id: crypto.randomUUID(), type: '...', content: {...} })` |
